_metadata.description: Mnemon's correction ledger. Single source of truth for all user corrections. Overrides stale data from ingested documents.

rules.rule: EXISTING CUSTOMER vs LEAD: If a company has EVER received a delivered machine from Machinecraft, they are an EXISTING CUSTOMER, not a lead. Even if they send a new inquiry, the new inquiry is a SEPARATE LEAD — but the company itself is a customer. Example: IAC Group is a past customer (1 machine delivered) AND has a new lead (2nd machine inquiry). Track both separately.
rules.triggered_by: IAC Group misclassified as only a lead

rules.rule: AGENTS vs LEADS: Mikhail Zraychenko (Russia) and Gerwin Pelle (Netherlands) are Machinecraft SALES AGENTS. They send inquiries ON BEHALF of end customers. Never list an agent as a lead. Always identify WHO the agent is asking for. Example: Mikhail sends inquiry for Domani-Spa (Vladimir) — the lead is Domani-Spa, not Mikhail.
rules.triggered_by: Mikhail and Gerwin listed as leads

rules.rule: VENDORS vs LEADS: Companies that supply services or parts TO Machinecraft are VENDORS, not customers. Never quote them a machine. Examples: iM Technologies (CAD design), apra-plast (product management), Siemens (component supplier).
rules.triggered_by: iM Technologies and Siemens listed as customers

rules.rule: QUOTE VERIFICATION: Only classify as PROPOSAL stage if there is EVIDENCE a quote was actually sent — PDF attachment, explicit pricing in email body, or quote number. Mere email discussion does NOT equal proposal sent. Example: eco-tad and Penda Plastic had discussions but no quote was ever sent.
rules.triggered_by: eco-tad and Penda Plastic listed as PROPOSAL leads

rules.rule: SISTER COMPANY SEPARATION: Formpack (Alok Doshi) is a SISTER COMPANY, not Machinecraft. Leads belonging to Formpack: TVS Motor, Dashmesh, EKA, TAFE, Reform. These must NEVER appear in Machinecraft pipeline or order book. Formpack also has its own machine orders FROM Machinecraft (PF1-C-3020, FCS XL) — those are valid Machinecraft orders.
rules.triggered_by: Dashmesh, EKA, TAFE, Reform, TVS listed as Machinecraft orders/leads

rules.rule: COMPANY VIABILITY: Before including a company in an active lead list, verify it still exists. Use web search (Iris agent). Example: Menschik GmbH no longer exists — quotes sent long ago are irrelevant.

STALE DATA OVERRIDE: Corrections from the user ALWAYS override data from ingested documents (spreadsheets, PDFs, KB chunks). Old spreadsheets may show 'Paint + Final Assy' for Dutch Tides when it's actually delivered. The correction ledger is the final source of truth. Mnemon must intercept stale data at every retrieval point.
rules.triggered_by: Dutch Tides and K2025 repeatedly shown with old status despite corrections

rules.rule: PAYMENT STATUS VERIFICATION: Do not report pending payments from old spreadsheets without verifying against recent emails. Payments may have been cleared. Example: JPCA, Zylog, IAC, Thermic had no pending payments despite the order book showing them.
rules.triggered_by: JPCA, Zylog, IAC, Thermic listed with pending payments

rules.rule: COMPLETED vs ACTIVE: Machines that are delivered and installed are COMPLETED PROJECTS, not active orders. Do not list them in the active order book. Example: Dutch Tides (delivered), K2025/Dezet (installed), JoPlast (delivered and paid), Plastisart (delivered).
rules.triggered_by: Delivered machines listed as active orders

rules.rule: OLD PROJECTS: Projects completed more than 1 year ago should not appear in current reports unless specifically asked for. Example: ALP Bedliner was completed 2 years ago.

MULTIPLE PROJECTS PER CUSTOMER: Some customers have multiple concurrent projects. Track each separately. Example: NCC Russia has 2 projects, Pinnacle has PF1-X-5028 (FAT) + PF1-A-2515 (fabrication), Venkateshwar has PF1-A-3020 + RT-4A 3020.

MACHINE STATUS ACCURACY: Always use the LATEST status from the Project Status Sheet, not from the Order Book (which may be months old). FAT > Fabrication > Design. Example: Pinnacle is in FAT (not fabrication), Jaquar PF1-X-3030 is installed (not PF1-W).
rules.triggered_by: Pinnacle shown as fabrication when actually in FAT

rules.rule: NAFFCO HAS MULTIPLE ENTITIES: Naffco UAE (Orwa Khabir, HDPE Benching $290K) and Naffco Saudi are SEPARATE projects. Track both. Also Naffco Flow Control (Ahmed Elbaroudy) is a related but different entity.

USE ALL AVAILABLE DATA SOURCES: When data is missing, think 'where can I find this?' Sources in priority order: (1) Email threads (search_emails), (2) PO/quote PDFs in archive (Alexandros read_file), (3) Spreadsheets in imports (MCT Orders, Order Book, MC Deadlines), (4) Knowledge base (Clio), (5) Web search (Iris). NEVER leave a field blank without trying all sources.
rules.triggered_by: Reports with '—' and 'TBD' when data existed in PO files

rules.rule: MISSED LEADS SOURCE: The Single Station Inquiry Form (Responses).xlsx in data/imports/02_Orders_and_POs/ contains ALL inbound inquiries from the website/exhibitions. Cross-reference this with sent quotes to find missed leads. Also check inbound emails to contact@machinecraft.org and sales@machinecraft.org.
rules.triggered_by: Missed leads section was too shallow

rules.rule: KTX IS A MACHINE SALE: KTX Japan (Hidehiro Uno) is a direct machine sale (new type IMG Press), NOT a strategic partnership. Treat as active order.

JAQUAR HISTORY: Jaquar has 2 machines — Machine 1 from 2015 and PF1-X-3030 for spas (2025, installed). The old order book may show PF1-W 3000x3000 which is the same machine. They have outstanding service punch list items.
rules.triggered_by: Jaquar shown with unclear machine history

rules.rule: ARRDEV IS IN PRODUCTION: Arrdev Pools machine is currently being built (in production), NOT complete or delivered. They are an active client.
